name: Operational State Summative Scoring Uses Synthetic Responses as Limited Training Augmentation
statement: The 2025 New Mexico MSSA technical report provides direct operational state summative evidence that LLM-generated synthetic responses can be used in a controlled, limited training-augmentation role for automated Writing scoring when high-score real examples are sparse, provided that generated responses are reviewed, approved, and the resulting scoring models are evaluated on independent validation sets.
population_or_context: New Mexico MSSA 2024-2025 state summative assessment automated scoring for English non-accommodated operational forms.
time_horizon: Full text retrieved and extracted on 2026-06-03.
quantitative_note: Approximately 100 synthetic responses were generated for each affected item, typically 10-30 approved per item; target training coverage was 2,000 responses per item and at least 200 responses per score point, with about 85% training and 15% validation. Model criteria included human-machine QWK >= 0.65, QWK degradation < 0.10, and |SMD| < 0.15.
caveats: The evidence supports training augmentation, not synthetic-only validation.
The report does not provide prompt details, generator identity, or subgroup fairness analysis for the synthetic responses.
The validation-set composition relative to synthetic responses is not fully specified in the extracted section.
evaluation_method: Review and approval of generated responses followed by independent validation-set model evaluation against professional-rater agreement criteria.
reported_metrics: Human-machine QWK, human-human minus human-machine QWK degradation, and human-machine standardized mean score difference.
limitations: Bounded to New Mexico MSSA automated Writing scoring and English non-accommodated operational forms.
Does not establish a broad vendor or assessment-policy norm for all high-stakes assessments.
Does not address cut scores, subgroup fairness validation, or live scoring without real-response validation.

source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: The finding paraphrases the training-data augmentation workflow, review/approval process, 85/15 split, and model evaluation criteria.
